title: Ctenitis arthrothrix
taxonomy: Ctenitis arthrothrix is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Ctenitis, which is a group of ferns native to Southeast Asia. Within the family Dryopteridaceae, Ctenitis arthrothrix is placed in the subfamily Dryopteridoideae. The classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and venation pattern. Further research may be needed to confirm its exact classification within the fern family.
uses: Ctenitis arthrothrix has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its extracts have shown potential in reducing inflammation and as an antioxidant. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in treating certain infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand and verify its medicinal uses. Traditional healers have used Ctenitis arthrothrix for a range of purposes, including treating fever and wounds.
